title: Harness contract and completion oracle
problem: Vean has many useful checks but no canonical tier taxonomy, aggregate runner, structured result, or machine-readable truth oracle.
target_invariant: Every non-meta harness claim maps to a versioned scenario ledger, immutable evidence envelope, executable sensitivity control, and profile-aware aggregate; the evaluator itself is proven independently against a fixed evidence corpus.
implementation_outline: Define immutable evidence envelope: source/tree/lock/config/fixture/command/artifact hashes, clean-state flag, runtime image, timestamps, and parent run/artifact IDs.
Define structured sensitivity controls with setup mutation, before/after hash, expected reason code, cleanup, and pass-fail-pass restoration.
Add versioned scenario manifests and aggregate profiles: developer, macos, release-candidate, release; implicit skips are invalid.
Implement verify:* orchestration and evaluator for downstream claims only.
Prove the evaluator independently by executing it against a fixed valid/invalid evidence corpus.
negative_controls: Fixed corpus includes stale SHA/tree, dirty inputs, substituted artifact, no-op mutation, wrong failure reason, missing scenario ID, implicit skip, and stale result.
pm_escalations: Any change to product claims beyond harness evidence naming.
Any proposal to mark a skipped platform tier as passed.
worker_prompt: Build H00 exactly from the approved roadmap. Own only the command facade, oracle evaluator, contract tests, and manifest plumbing. Do not implement downstream test suites. Return changed paths, command output, and negative-control output.
completion_criteria: Meta evaluator contract passes independently.
Every downstream claim has executable command/scenario/control or remains open.
Aggregate profile membership and skip policy are explicit.
Stale/substituted/no-op evidence cannot produce verified.
risks: The facade could become a second test implementation; constrain it to orchestration and result normalization.

title: Close static workspace gates
problem: Owned viewer/Remotion/Rust code is outside normal static verification.
target_invariant: All owned TS/TSX and platform-neutral plus macOS-target Rust code is typechecked, linted/formatted, and build-checked on the correct target.
implementation_outline: Add viewer-specific strict typecheck with DOM and worker types.
Bring owned viewer and Remotion source under Biome with intentional generated/vendor exclusions only.
Add Cargo fmt, clippy, and test commands.
Make viewer production build a separate static gate.
Run macOS-target Cargo check/clippy/test on a pinned macOS toolchain; Linux-only static proof cannot satisfy macOS code claims.
negative_controls: Introduce a viewer-only type error and prove verify:fast fails.
Introduce a Rust warning in an all-target test module and prove clippy fails.
Seed code behind a macOS cfg and require the macOS static lane to fail while Linux remains green.
pm_escalations: Generated Remotion code requiring a documented exclusion.
Static failures revealing architectural browser/global conflicts.
worker_prompt: Implement H01 after H00. Own workspace configs and package scripts only; make minimal source fixes required by newly honest gates and report each.
completion_criteria: No owned production workspace is excluded silently.
Fast gate fails on viewer and Rust defects.
Existing Remotion/React pin parity remains enforced.
risks: Turning on gates may reveal real latent errors; fix concrete errors rather than weakening config.

title: Hermetic fixtures and process lifecycle
problem: Tests can share real repo/user state and child processes/ports; loopback mutation authority is not launch-scoped.
target_invariant: Every run behaviorally proves isolated HOME/project/DB/socket ownership and launch authority; an independent watchdog finds and reaps tagged descendants even after reparenting.
implementation_outline: Create one secret-safe fixture descriptor; store authority material in a mode-0600 sidecar file or inherited descriptor, not the general result manifest.
Poison developer-state values and use canary records/inode/socket-owner checks to prove children consume isolated state rather than merely report it.
Launch all processes with a run marker, PGID, executable/start tuple, owned-port ledger, and an independent outer watchdog.

Black-box every mutating/process-starting route against the bound production-mode server for valid, cross-origin, DNS-rebinding Host, missing/null Origin, form request, wrong token, and prior-launch replay cases.
Define separate dev/test/release CSP and navigation policy; verify response headers and effective policy without granting production remote Tauri authority.
Scan logs, URLs, screenshots, results, and persisted files for leaked authority material.
negative_controls: Run two conflicting canary writes and verify separate DB inodes and canonical developer-state hashes unchanged.
Seed a detached/reparenting marked child and require watchdog detection.
Replay an old launch token and send form-encoded/cross-origin/DNS-rebinding mutations; require uniform rejection before body parsing.
Attempt unexpected navigation/resource/eval under release policy and require blocking.
pm_escalations: Any authentication design that changes public CLI/MCP behavior.
Platform-specific process-group semantics that require a product decision.
worker_prompt: Implement H02 as the shared hermetic substrate. Preserve the action registry and HTTP media architecture. Do not add UI tests. Prove unauthorized mutation and orphan-process negative controls.
completion_criteria: Behavior, not descriptors alone, proves isolation.
Independent watchdog reports no marked process, owned socket, or open fixture file after every exit mode.
All mutation routes enforce launch authority and no secret leaks.
Release webview policy is enforced and csp:null cannot satisfy the claim.
risks: Security and harness concerns can sprawl; keep the token local and transport-neutral.

title: Early CI bootstrap
problem: Deferring CI until M3 leaves H00-H08 implementation unprotected.
target_invariant: Immediately after H00, push-main CI runs current fast/oracle checks and grows by calling newly merged canonical commands; it never duplicates scenarios.
implementation_outline: Create push-main/workflow-dispatch workflow for current fast/meta checks.
Use reusable jobs whose command list is derived from the truth manifest/profile.
Add cleanup/evidence upload skeleton and workflow hash to evidence envelope.
Expand required tiers after each milestone merge.
negative_controls: Removed trigger, continue-on-error, skipped required job, and stale workflow hash fail CI policy validation.
pm_escalations: Runner availability/cost only.
worker_prompt: PM bootstraps CI immediately after H00. Call canonical commands only. Extend incrementally after each milestone.
completion_criteria: M0 changes are automatically gated on push main.
CI policy/evidence binds to workflow hash.
risks: Main-only workflow cannot protect pre-merge private worktrees; workers still run local gates and PM proves after integration.

title: Freeze timeline keyboard and accessibility contract
problem: The roadmap could invent easy keyboard behavior and call the timeline accessible before the product interaction contract exists.
target_invariant: A versioned, Tejas-approved interaction contract defines focus, roles/names, selection, move/trim/blade modifiers, announcements, undo/redo, pointer parity, and focus restoration.
implementation_outline: Document current pointer gestures and platform conventions.
Propose complete keyboard/focus/announcement contract with representative workflows.
Obtain user approval and freeze version/hash for H03/H06/H08 evidence.
negative_controls: No automated timeline-accessibility claim may verify while contract status is unapproved or hash mismatches.
pm_escalations: Tejas approves product interaction semantics.
worker_prompt: PM prepares and presents the timeline keyboard/accessibility contract; do not implement UI before approval.
completion_criteria: Contract approved and hashed.
H03/H06 scenario ledgers reference exact contract version.
risks: This is the one intentional product-decision gate in the component program.

title: Real-browser React component suite
problem: React components and interaction semantics have no dedicated test runner, and jsdom would give false confidence for visual/browser behavior.
target_invariant: View logic and component contracts run in an actual browser with semantic locators, keyboard coverage, StrictMode cleanup, and deterministic mocked action adapters.
implementation_outline: Configure Vitest Browser Mode with a driver provider and pinned browser.
Add semantic component tests for panels, transport, timeline selection/keyboard, errors, loading, and undo/redo.
Use mocks only at viewer API/action boundaries; unmocked actions fail loudly.
Run under React StrictMode and fail on unhandled console errors.
Bind scenario ledger to the approved timeline interaction contract and independently verify canonical .mlt/IR for a representative keyboard edit.
negative_controls: Remove a control's accessible name and require a role/name query to fail.
Leave an effect listener mounted across StrictMode remount and require cleanup test failure.
Call an unregistered action and require the test to fail.
Update DOM/focus without executing the canonical edit action; require document-truth failure.
pm_escalations: Timeline keyboard interaction product semantics requiring user choice.
Any temptation to mock WebCodecs/WebGL and call it media proof.
worker_prompt: Implement H03 on the real-browser component boundary. Own viewer tests/config only. Do not duplicate pure algorithms already covered by root Vitest or claim media/Tauri coverage.
completion_criteria: Key component states and keyboard paths are semantic and deterministic.
No jsdom claim covers browser media/layout.
Accessibility regressions have negative controls.
risks: Component tests can overfit markup; query by user semantics and domain outcomes.

title: Browser editor E2E and agent-proof convergence
problem: Strong loopback/agent-browser assets are standalone and ephemeral rather than a repeatable browser E2E suite with structured evidence.
target_invariant: The real preview server/browser editor has a versioned scenario ledger whose mutating flows independently prove action ID/input/envelope, touched URI, canonical .mlt hash/parsed IR, diagnostics, DOM semantics, and cleanup.
implementation_outline: Wrap existing live scenarios in a shared WDIO/browser runner and result schema.
Preserve drive lifecycle/session safety and make it consume H02 fixture descriptors.
Assert DOM plus timeline/action/diagnostic truth, not screenshots alone.
Capture screenshot, DOM snapshot, logs, and short recording on failure.
Keep HMR as a dev-only contract and add separate production-dist proof.
For every mutating scenario, independently parse the persisted .mlt after the UI action; viewer stores and screenshots are never the document oracle.
Version the scenario ledger with required IDs, fixtures, engine, assertions, forbidden substitutions, negative controls, and artifact set.
negative_controls: Serve a stale dist and require production-build freshness check to fail.
Force a Remotion composition error and require editor survival plus attributable error.
Leak a drive process and require teardown audit to fail.
Update visible timeline without executing/persisting the action and require failure.
Persist the expected edit to a different timeline URI and require touched-URI/current-document failure.
pm_escalations: Changes that would replace agent-browser as a demonstration tool without equivalent agent UX.
worker_prompt: Implement H04 after fixture/component contracts. Consolidate existing live scripts without deleting their substantive negative cases. Return scenario mapping and failure artifacts.
completion_criteria: Existing live scenarios are discoverable through one command.
Browser proof includes domain assertions and teardown.
Agent drive skill reflects the canonical harness.
Executed scenario IDs exactly match the required ledger version.
Every mutation proves canonical document and diagnostic truth.
risks: Runner migration can lose specialized scenarios; maintain a one-to-one migration ledger.

title: Embedded Tauri/WKWebView safe-path spike and suite
problem: Vean has no actual WKWebView E2E, and its localhost navigation conflicts with the stock advanced-WDIO recipe.
target_invariant: One of two predeclared safe branches proves the actual Tauri process/final WKWebView lifecycle: least-privilege embedded DOM control, or the H05F black-box fallback; production instrumentation absence is scanned against the final release lineage.
implementation_outline: Pin @wdio/tauri-service and both Rust plugins at the verified compatibility set.
First test embedded WebDriver DOM control after Vean's real localhost navigation without elevated frontend plugin authority.
If advanced APIs are required, add a fixed-origin, feature-gated test viewer mode and minimal permissions only.
Run serially with explicit binary path, unique WebDriver port, backend/frontend logs, and explicit process pre/post cleanup.
Add release inspection proving no plugin registration, capability, environment listener, or open WebDriver port.
Externally bind evidence to app PID/bundle ID, binary hash/build profile, session capabilities, macOS/WebKit version, final localhost URL, and canonical action/document result.
If safe embedded control fails, terminate this branch with reason and dispatch H05F; do not dead-end downstream graph.
negative_controls: Standalone-browser substitution must fail Tauri PID/bundle/window predicates.
Splash-only instrumentation must fail final-viewer scenario ledger.
A release-profile mutant with one WDIO feature/capability enabled must be detected by the same static/dynamic scanner.
Exact final signed app must reject session creation on all app-owned loopback listeners.
pm_escalations: If basic embedded control fails and advanced plugin requires unsafe production authority, stop and retain browser + Appium tiers instead.
Any request to enable withGlobalTauri or WDIO permissions in production.
worker_prompt: Run H05 as a spike before committing broad Tauri E2E. Prefer least privilege. Record both successful and failed configurations. Do not ship test plugins. Stop at the escalation boundary if localhost navigation cannot be instrumented safely.
completion_criteria: Safe branch decision recorded with evidence.
Actual Tauri process and final viewer are externally identified.
Embedded success or H05F fallback provides required lifecycle evidence.
Final release lineage is free of test authority.
risks: Young integration and localhost navigation may fail; roadmap has a non-blocking fallback.

title: WKWebView black-box fallback
problem: A safe embedded DOM path may fail; prose-only fallback would dead-end media/package dependencies.
target_invariant: Without remote Tauri authority, Mac2/LaunchServices plus launch-scoped in-app self-test telemetry prove final viewer load, user gesture, media/action result, process lifecycle, and nonblank/error state in actual WKWebView.
implementation_outline: Trigger exact fixture workflow through native accessibility/user input.
Emit bounded signed/run-scoped telemetry to an isolated file/stdio channel, not remote Tauri authority.
Externally bind PID/bundle/binary/final URL and independently verify action/.mlt/media outcome.
Feed H07 actual-WKWebView matrix with self-test results.
negative_controls: Standalone browser and splash-only runs fail PID/final-URL binding.
Synthetic telemetry with wrong run nonce/artifact hash rejects.
Black frame or stalled media fails nonblank/progress/domain predicates.
pm_escalations: If neither safe embedded nor bounded black-box evidence can prove required Tauri behavior, affected claims remain blocked_with_user_decision.
worker_prompt: Execute only if H05 safe embedded branch fails. Do not enable production remote Tauri authority. Build the smallest external/self-test proof sufficient for lifecycle and media claims.
completion_criteria: Fallback command is executable and scenario-ledger bound.
Downstream H07/H08 can consume exact actual-app evidence.
No test listener/authority ships.
risks: Black-box evidence is narrower than DOM control; claim wording must remain narrow.

title: Pre-register media matrix, goldens, and performance budgets
problem: Thresholds, required runtime cells, and goldens chosen after results allow post-hoc passes.
target_invariant: Before acceptance, versioned policies freeze media fixtures/matrix outcomes, golden provenance, performance environments/metrics/warmup/samples/percentiles/threshold approval, and live-export semantic parity cases.
implementation_outline: Measure baseline distributions without acceptance thresholds.
Define required supported/unsupported/fallback cells for exact Chrome/WKWebView/OS/hardware/codecs.
Freeze golden generation provenance and separate generate vs compare commands.
Define curated live/export parity frames and semantic markers.
Present performance budgets to Tejas for approval and hash policy.
negative_controls: Acceptance command refuses unapproved or post-dated policy.
Required unsupported cell blocks rather than shrinking denominator.
pm_escalations: Tejas approves performance budgets and any required unsupported runtime cell.
worker_prompt: Measure and propose policies only; do not mark media claims verified. Return distributions, required matrix, golden provenance, and approval questions.
completion_criteria: Policies are approved, versioned, and hashed.
Acceptance cannot regenerate goldens or thresholds.
risks: Hardware variability requires scoped claims, not global performance language.

title: Native macOS surface and accessibility smoke
problem: WebView drivers cannot prove AppKit menus, Finder dialogs, window behavior, or the native accessibility tree.
target_invariant: A small serialized Mac2 suite proves the native shell surfaces without becoming the main product E2E runner.
implementation_outline: Configure WDIO/Appium 3/Mac2 with a doctor command and documented Accessibility requirements.
Drive File menu, project-open panel cancel/select, window close/reopen if supported, and quit.
Assert native accessibility labels/roles for shell-owned controls.
Run maxInstances=1 and reserve for nightly/release or a prepared Mac lane.
negative_controls: Remove a menu item label and require the accessibility locator to fail.
Open a panel and leave it unresolved; require teardown to fail on residual app/dialog state.
pm_escalations: Hosted-runner permission limitations; choose self-hosted/manual release lane rather than weakening proof.
worker_prompt: Implement only the bounded native-surface smoke. Do not attempt to drive the detailed web editor through Mac2. Return setup doctor output, scenario evidence, and cleanup proof.
completion_criteria: Native menu/dialog/window scenarios are automated on a prepared Mac.
Suite is serialized and hermetic.
DOM accessibility remains in H03/H05, not duplicated.
risks: macOS permissions and XCTest signing can be operationally brittle; keep suite thin.

title: Media, Remotion, resilience, and performance oracles
problem: Current media checks are valuable but do not systematically separate live Player behavior, actual codec/runtime support, exported pixels, recovery, and performance budgets.
target_invariant: Versioned policies prove declared live-media behavior on exact runtimes, deterministic render fidelity, live/export semantic parity, owned-handle cleanup and stress resilience, plus debug-CI and release-package performance budgets.
implementation_outline: Consume pre-approved H07B policies; acceptance never regenerates goldens or thresholds.
For each live cell decode exact fixture bytes and assert timestamps/seek, nonblank content signature, audio analyser/offline signal where in scope, and specified fallback where unsupported.
Prove Player buffering/waiting/resume/seek/error separately from renderStill readiness.
At curated frames bind one canonical .mlt/IR frame, composition ID, props/assets/version hashes and compare live/export semantic markers without requiring cross-engine byte identity.
Track application-owned VideoFrame/ImageBitmap/decoder/worker/context handles deterministically; separately stress actual runtime bounds.
Measure browser-CI and optimized release-package performance separately with bounded telemetry and recorded instrumentation overhead.
negative_controls: Stalled buffer, corrupt chunk, unsupported profile, wrong seek timestamp, silent/wrong-channel audio.
One-frame live mapping offset, export-only prop change, and divergent asset resolution must fail semantic parity.
Known render transform/color/layer/stream/boundary defects must fail approved golden/stream regions.
Remove one owned-handle close and require ledger failure even if browser reclaims memory; inject context loss and require valid post-recovery content.
Injected long task/cache leak must cross pre-approved Chrome and release-WKWebView budgets.
pm_escalations: Budget values require baseline measurement and user-visible product judgment.
Codec absence on hosted hardware must be reported as unsupported evidence, never passed via mocks.
worker_prompt: Implement H07 only after both browser and WKWebView lanes exist. Preserve export/live separation. Establish baselines before setting thresholds and include negative controls for buffering, resource cleanup, context loss, and latency.
completion_criteria: Live and export readiness are separate.
Actual runtime media paths run in Chrome and WKWebView.
Budgets are measured and enforced with distributions, not one sample.
risks: Hardware variance can make global thresholds flaky; pin environment and use robust percentiles.

title: Self-contained unsigned/package-candidate proof
problem: A source-tree/native test build can pass while the downloaded app still needs Bun, source, Homebrew, user state, or unsigned/unnotarized artifacts.
target_invariant: An exact hashed DMG/package candidate contains the Vean core and sidecars and passes LaunchServices clean-environment smoke on a clean VM/user with checkout unmounted, no Bun/Homebrew, empty HOME, and process/network/filesystem evidence.
implementation_outline: Choose/freeze packaged core format without porting the core to Rust.
Emit immutable package BOM for core, viewer assets, sidecars, Tauri executable, app, and DMG candidate.
Install through DMG/LaunchServices in a clean VM/ephemeral user with checkout unavailable, quarantine applied, no Bun/Homebrew/caches.
Record loaded/opened paths, network, created state, project open, play/scrub, still/video hashes, quit, and post-quit process/port scan.
Hand exact candidate BOM to PM-owned H08R; ordinary worker receives no signing/updater secrets.
negative_controls: Inject an absolute source dependency and require clean host failure.
Remove core service or one dylib/resource and require package preflight failure.
Attempt same-host scrubbed run and ensure it cannot satisfy clean-machine claim.
pm_escalations: Choice of compiled core format and distribution architecture.
Apple credential/key custody and updater key backup.
worker_prompt: Treat H08 as a release program, not a build-success check. Start with a format decision/spec, preserve GPL sidecar boundaries, and prove the installed artifact under a scrubbed environment.
completion_criteria: Exact candidate hash works on clean host via LaunchServices.
BOM closes source-to-DMG candidate lineage.
No signing/updater/manual-accessibility overclaim.
risks: Packaging Bun/dynamic composition assets may expose hidden runtime dependencies; inventory and fail closed.

title: Canonical release, signing, notarization, updater, and manual accessibility
problem: Secret-bearing release proof and manual accessibility cannot be owned by an ordinary package worker, and independent green artifacts can refer to different binaries.
target_invariant: PM closes one immutable release lineage from source/core/assets/sidecars through signed app, notarized/stapled DMG, updater payload, installed smoke, packaged Mac2 smoke, and current manual VoiceOver/keyboard assessment.
implementation_outline: Create release BOM/lineage with hashes and parent relationships for every nested/final artifact.
Sign/notarize/staple, then rerun clean installed-app and bounded Mac2 smoke against exact final DMG hash.
Run end-to-end vN to vN+1 production updater flow with relaunch/state preservation/failure atomicity and tamper/replay/downgrade controls.
Perform current keyboard-only and VoiceOver assessment across AppKit/WKWebView using exact package hash.
negative_controls: Mutant lineage with substituted app/DMG/updater hash rejects.
Tampered metadata/payload, interrupted install, downgrade/replay leave vN runnable.
Packaged-only broken menu resource fails Mac2 package smoke.
Manual checklist cannot attach to a different package/OS/VoiceOver version.
pm_escalations: Apple/updater key custody and release authority.
Manual accessibility assessor availability.
worker_prompt: PM-owned secret-bearing release lane. Consume H08 unsigned/package candidate, create exact lineage, rerun final artifact smoke, updater flow, and manual accessibility. Never expose secrets to worker lanes.
completion_criteria: One closed release lineage satisfies all release claims.
Exact final artifact is smoked after signing/notarization.
Updater is end-to-end, not signature-only.
Manual accessibility evidence is current and package-bound.
risks: Release credentials and prepared Mac are external gates; blocked claims cannot count complete.

title: CI orchestration and failure evidence
problem: No automated system runs the layered gates or retains failure evidence.
target_invariant: CI policy maps claims to required jobs and actual current-main/release-lineage runs; per-tier controlled failures prove real cleanup and evidence retrieval.
implementation_outline: Finalize profile-to-workflow/job/trigger/runner/secret policy with workflow blob hashes.
Query actual GitHub check runs for current main SHA and exact release lineage; skipped/cancelled/neutral/stale/wrong-artifact runs do not verify.
Run controlled reason-coded failure in each integration tier's actual CI job and independently retrieve/validate uploaded evidence.
Enforce repository/environment concurrency for Mac2/release lanes across runs, not only maxInstances inside one job.
Keep secret-bearing release job PM-owned and isolated.
negative_controls: Correct YAML mapping with no run, previous-SHA green run, wrong-DMG green run, removed trigger, continue-on-error, skipped job, stale workflow hash.
Each tier omits one required evidence artifact in a controlled failure and remains unverified.
pm_escalations: Runner cost/availability changes.
Any proposal to expose Apple or updater secrets to ordinary test jobs.
worker_prompt: Implement H09 only after canonical commands exist. Workflows orchestrate them and must not contain alternate test logic. Prove failure artifact upload and cleanup with a controlled failing run.
completion_criteria: Every manifest claim has an assigned CI/manual lane.
Push-main gates are automatic.
Release secrets are isolated.
Failure evidence is retrievable.
risks: CI YAML can drift from local commands; workflows must call the same verify:* facade.

title: Guidance, ownership, and completion convergence
problem: Current docs correctly describe browser proof but do not encode the new layered boundaries, production exclusion rules, or completion oracle.
target_invariant: AGENTS, roadmap, skills, and contributor docs teach one accurate harness with no duplicate or stale completion claims.
implementation_outline: Update the closest existing guidance rather than adding parallel doctrine.
Define which tier is required for each change class.
Document test-only WDIO security and release negative controls.
Document evidence artifacts and escalation to Mac2/package lanes.
Add drift tests for command names and skills catalog.
Replace absolute line-number references in mutable guidance with stable anchors or drift-checked command IDs.
Teach success and fallback WDIO branches, scenario/profile vocabulary, artifact lineage, and manual accessibility boundaries.
negative_controls: Reference a removed command in a skill and require drift test failure.
Mark a browser-only result as package proof and require oracle rejection.
pm_escalations: None unless product completion policy changes.
worker_prompt: Integrate final observed harness truth into existing guidance after all commands land. Remove contradicted text, add drift enforcement, and avoid duplicating the roadmap.
completion_criteria: One canonical tier/command vocabulary.
Drive skill remains agent-friendly and accurately bounded.
Docs cannot claim done from the wrong evidence tier.
risks: Documentation can overgrow; prefer tables, command contracts, and enforced drift tests.
